data.title: Kingsway signals accelerated M&A with new capital, targets 3–5 acquisitions per year amid KSX momentum
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Kingsway Financial Services Inc. (KFS) Q2 2025

Management View

  • John Taylor-Maloney Fitzgerald, CEO, announced that "the second quarter of 2025 marked a major inflection point for the company." He emphasized Kingsway's unique position as "the only publicly traded U.S. company employing the search fund model to acquire and build great businesses." Highlighting a strategic shift, Fitzgerald stated, "our Board of Directors, with management support, made an exciting decision: we are ready to accelerate growth."
  • Fitzgerald reported a private placement (PIPE transaction) with five institutional investors, contributing $15.7 million. He explained that this capital, combined with cash flow and other sources, will "provide Kingsway with the financial resources to scale faster and to deliver the company's multiyear growth ambitions."
  • The company raised its KSX acquisition target from 2–3 to 3–5 per year, citing increased confidence in the model and a growing pipeline.
  • Since completing the PIPE, Fitzgerald detailed the execution of three acquisitions: Roundhouse Electric & Equipment Co. for $22.4 million, AAA Flexible Pipe Cleaning Corp (Advanced Plumbing and Drain) for up to $5 million, and The HR Team as a tuck-in for Ravix Group. Each acquisition aligns with the capital-light, recurring revenue criteria central to Kingsway's strategy.
  • Fitzgerald reported that Kingsway's trailing 12-month adjusted run rate EBITDA for the businesses owned as of quarter end stands at approximately $22 million to $23 million.
  • Kent A. Hansen, CFO, stated, "For the second quarter, consolidated revenue was $30.9 million, an increase of 16.9% compared to $26.4 million in the second quarter of 2024." He added, "Consolidated adjusted EBITDA was $1.7 million for the 3 months ended June 30, 2025, compared to $2.5 million in the prior year quarter."
  • Hansen reported KSX segment revenue of $13.3 million, up 42.1% year-over-year, and adjusted EBITDA of $2.4 million, up 31%. He cited both organic growth and recent acquisitions as drivers.
  • Hansen addressed the Extended Warranty segment: "Revenue increased by 3.1% to $17.6 million in the second quarter, up from $17.1 million in the prior year period. Adjusted EBITDA was down to $600,000 from $1.6 million in the prior year quarter."
  • Hansen disclosed a $600,000 expense tied to a legal settlement, clarifying "this expense will not recur going forward."

Outlook

  • Fitzgerald stated, "We currently have 2 Operators-in-Residence, or OIRs, who are actively searching for our next acquisition targets, and we are in the process of interviewing high-quality candidates to expand this bench."
  • The company increased its target range for KSX acquisitions per year to 3–5 and has acquired 5 asset-light services businesses year-to-date.
  • No explicit forward-looking financial guidance was provided in the transcript.

Financial Results

  • Consolidated revenue for Q2 2025 was $30.9 million, up 16.9% year-over-year.
  • Consolidated adjusted EBITDA was $1.7 million for the quarter, compared to $2.5 million in the prior year period.
  • KSX segment revenue was $13.3 million, up 42.1%, and adjusted EBITDA was $2.4 million, up 31% from the prior year.
  • Extended Warranty segment revenue increased to $17.6 million, with adjusted EBITDA declining to $600,000.
  • Net debt at quarter end was $46.3 million, decreasing from $52 million at year-end, mainly due to PIPE proceeds.
  • The company recorded a $600,000 nonrecurring legal settlement expense.

Q&A

  • Christian Solberg, Sun Mountain Partners, asked if the reported $22 million to $23 million run rate EBITDA included recent acquisitions. Fitzgerald confirmed it did and explained the breakdown when subtracting contribution from new deals.
  • Solberg asked about the decline in run rate EBITDA excluding new acquisitions compared to Q1; Fitzgerald attributed the shortfall to "warranty had a pretty tough year-over-year comp on a GAAP basis."
  • James Carbonara, Hayden IR, asked if Kingsway would cease acquisitions after reaching its target. Fitzgerald responded, "No, I don't think that that's a good expectation...we currently have 2 OIRs actively looking for our next acquisition...we're not going to go pencils down once we hit some internal subjective target."
  • Carbonara inquired about OIR pipeline; Fitzgerald said, "we certainly expect this number to increase. We'd like to get back up to our kind of normal 4 to 5, and we have a great pipeline."
  • On acquisition evolution, Fitzgerald explained a "focus on revenue quality, with a higher standard of recurring revenue being primary."
  • Regarding OIR hiring, Fitzgerald stated, "we have a set of criteria, a set of attributes, that we think are important...we're going to continue to screen based on our criteria."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts' tone was neutral, with questions focused on acquisition pacing, financial metrics, and operational strategy.
  • Management maintained a confident and optimistic tone in both prepared remarks and responses, with Fitzgerald stating, "The energy at Kingsway is palpable, and I look forward to sharing more good news in the second half of 2025."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both management and analysts exhibited increased confidence and engagement, reflecting the company's active acquisition strategy and capital raise.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter featured a significant capital raise ($15.7 million PIPE) and an increase in the annual KSX acquisition target to 3–5, compared to 2–3 previously.
  • Five acquisitions year-to-date outpaced the pace discussed in Q1, signaling acceleration.
  • KSX run rate EBITDA rose to $22–23 million from $18–19 million, though excluding new acquisitions, the figure was lower due to Extended Warranty segment GAAP headwinds.
  • Analysts in the current quarter asked more detailed questions about acquisition strategy and run rate metrics, whereas the previous quarter focused more on platform strategy and leadership.
  • Management tone was more assertive and expansionary, reflecting increased momentum and capital resources.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management noted that the Extended Warranty segment had a "pretty tough year-over-year comp on a GAAP basis," impacting run rate EBITDA.
  • Hansen discussed a $600,000 expense tied to a legacy legal settlement, clarifying it is nonrecurring.
  • Management emphasized discipline in acquisitions, aiming for quality over simply hitting targets.
  • No new material risks were highlighted beyond sector performance and legacy legal costs.

Final Takeaway

Kingsway closed the quarter with a strengthened balance sheet, a higher annual acquisition target, and a record level of KSX segment earnings power. The company executed three acquisitions post-PIPE, reinforced its search fund strategy, and signaled ongoing acquisition activity for the remainder of 2025. Management described the second quarter as a major inflection point, with momentum building across platforms and a clear focus on scaling through disciplined, recurring-revenue acquisitions.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: SoftBank acquires Foxconn’s Ohio EV facility to potentially boost Stargate project
data.description:
SoftBank Group (OTCPK:SFTBY) (OTCPK:SFTBF) is the buyer taking ownership of Foxconn Technology's (OTCPK:FXCOF) electric vehicle plant in Ohio, a move intended to jump-start the company’s $500B Stargate data center project with OpenAI and Oracle (NYSE:ORCL), Bloomberg News reported.

SoftBank had approached the Apple (AAPL) supplier — which is formally known as Hon Hai Precision (OTCPK:HNHAF) (OTCPK:HNHPF) — with its plan to establish data centers and related infrastructure across the U.S., the report added, citing people with knowledge of the matter. The EV plant sale is a result of these efforts by the Japanese company.

Foxconn and SoftBank did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

Earlier this week, Hon Hai Precision Industry, the flagship unit of Foxconn, said it agreed to sell the EV plant to Crescent Dune LLC for $375M without disclosing the company behind the entity. SoftBank is Foxconn’s counterparty in the transaction, the report noted.

Foxconn would operate the plant and use the site for its own AI server manufacturing business, the report added. The Ohio facility may be used to host a data center.

The two companies, which collaborated to make SoftBank’s humanoid Pepper robots a decade ago, formed a joint venture this year to establish a data center and manufacturing venture in the U.S., with each investing $735M. It is not clear if SoftBank’s investment in the venture includes the $375M for the EV plant, the report added.

On Thursday, SoftBank's CFO Yoshimitsu Goto said it was taking longer than expected to get the Stargate project off the ground, acknowledging for the first time that the $500B AI collaboration in the U.S. with OpenAI is slowing down.

SoftBank's Founder Masayoshi Son and OpenAI Co-Founder Sam Altman introduced the Stargate project in January along with President Donald Trump, with promises to start deploying $100B “immediately” and raise that to about $500B.

Meanwhile, Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT)-backed OpenAI has proceeded with other data centers that use the Stargate brand. A project in Abilene, Texas, touted as Stargate’s first site, was on-track before Trump’s announcement or SoftBank’s involvement. The Japanese company was not involved in plans for a large Stargate data center in Norway, where OpenAI would be the anchor customer. OpenAI’s Stargate project also includes a 5-gigawatt data center complex in Abu Dhabi.

data.title: AMC Networks' streaming revenue contributes to Q2 beat
data.description:
AMC Networks (NASDAQ:AMCX) swung to a profit in the second quarter,  fueled by double digit increases in content licensing revenue, ad revenue, and streaming revenue, driving unadjusted income to $0.91 per share versus a loss of $0.66 in the same quarter last year.

On an adjusted basis, the company earned a profit of $0.69 per share, which was down 44% from a year ago but 8 cents above expectations. Net revenue was also down from a year ago to $600M but exceeded estimates by $17M.

The results drove shares higher in Friday’s premarket action with shares last traded with a gain of 2%.

“In the second quarter, we saw streaming revenue growth accelerate, strength in content licensing and continued healthy free cash flow generation. We are increasing our free cash flow outlook for 2025 and now expect approximately $250 million of free cash flow for the full year,” said Kristin Dolan, CEO of AMC Networks.

In the company’s domestic markets, a 2% increase in streaming subscriptions contributed to a 12% increase in streaming revenue. Subscription revenue and affiliate revenue were both down, however, by 1% and 12%, respectively, offset by a 26% improvement in content licensing revenue, resulting in a 2% decline in domestic revenue.

International revenue fell 16% due to lower subscription and advertising revenue.

On the balance sheet, AMC Networks (NASDAQ:AMCX) generated $95.7 million in free cash flow for the quarter, up 0.6% year-over-year. The company expects $250M of free cash flow for FY25.

data.title: American Axle & Manufacturing reports mixed Q2 results; updates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• American Axle & Manufacturing press release (NYSE:AXL): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.21 beats by $0.07.
  • Revenue of $1.54B (-5.5% Y/Y) misses by $30M.
  • Adjusted EBITDA of $202.2 million, or 13.2% of sales.
  • Net cash provided by operating activities of $91.9 million; Adjusted free cash flow of $48.7 million.

  • AAM's Adjusted free cash flow for the second quarter of 2025 was $48.7 million as compared to $97.9 million for the second quarter of 2024. 

    AAM's 2025 Updated Financial Outlook

    AAM's full year 2025 financial targets are as follows:    

    • AAM is targeting sales in the range of $5.75 - $5.95 billion vs. $5.65 - $5.95 billion prior vs. consensus of $5.83B.
    • AAM is targeting Adjusted EBITDA in the range of $695 - $745 million vs. $665 - $745 million prior.
    • AAM is targeting Adjusted free cash flow in the range of $175 - $215 million vs. $165 - $215 million prior; this target assumes capital spending of approximately 5% of sales.

    These targets are based on the following assumptions for 2025:

    • North American light vehicle production of approximately 14.6 - 15.1 million units.
    • AAM's production estimates of key programs that we support.
    • Excludes costs and expenses associated with the announced combination with Dowlais. Reflects AAM on a stand-alone pre-combination basis only.
    • No changes to USMCA.
    • Mitigation of a majority of incremental tariff costs.

data.title: MOL Magyar Olaj- és Gázipari Nyilvánosan Muködo Részvénytársaság reports Q2 results
data.description:

data.title: Wendy's trades lower after clipping full-year guidance amid sluggish trends in the U.S.
data.description:
The Wendy's Company (NASDAQ:WEN) fell in early trading on Friday after lowering its sales and profit guidance for the full year. 

For Q2, systemwide sales fell 1.8%, as a 3.3% decline in the U.S. was only partially offset by the 8.7% increase in the international business. Same-restaurant sales were down 2.9% during the month.

Adjusted EBITDA was reported at $146.6 million vs. $143.1 million a year ago. The increase in adjusted EBITDA was said to be primarily driven by a decrease in the company's investment in advertising spend, lower general and administrative expense, and higher net franchise fees. Those factors were partially offset by a decrease in franchise royalty revenue and a decrease in U.S. Company-operated restaurant margin. EPS rose to $0.29 in Q2 from $0.27 a year ago.

Wendy's (NASDAQ:WEN) continued to expand its global footprint in Q2 by adding 44 new restaurants to bring its total additions to 118 in the first half of the year. Interim CEO Ken Cook noted the company is encouraged by the strong momentum in the international business, which delivered 8.7% systemwide sales growth in the quarter and is seen as offering excellent opportunities for expansion.

Meanwhile, Wendy's (WEN) also plans to work to improve the U.S. business. "In the U.S., we have work to do to improve the overall performance of the business. We will continue to strengthen relationships with franchisees, improve the effectiveness of our marketing programs, and elevate the customer experience across the system.," highlighted Cook. "I'm confident that increasing our focus in these areas positions the company for stronger long-term performance," he added.

As for guidance, for the full year, Wendy's (WEN) sees global systemwide sales growth of -5% to -3% vs. a prior outlook for -2% to 0%. Full-year EPS of $0.82 to $0.89 (midpoint $0.855) is anticipated vs. a prior outlook for $0.92 to $0.94 and $0.94 consensus.

Shares of Wendy's (WEN) fell 3.8% in premarket trading.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: FuboTV beats Q2 top-line and bottom-line estimates
bpost NV/SA reports Q2 results
data.description:
• bpost NV/SA press release (OTCPK:BPOSF): Q2 adjusted EBIT at 58.3 mEUR with a margin of 5.3%
  • Operating income at 1,092.3 mEUR, +10.5% or +104.1 mEUR compared to last year
  • 2025 outlook
  • Year-to-date group results and adjusted group EBIT of 99.9 mEUR are broadly in line with expectations and track towards the full-year group EBIT guidance
  • The EBIT guidance of 150–180 mEUR, initially introduced in February 2025 and maintained in May at the Q1 results with a “reduced exposure to the lower end of the range” is reaffirmed

data.title: Heron Therapeutics GAAP EPS of -$0.02 misses by $0.01, revenue of $37.2M misses by $0.88M
data.description:

data.title: Doximity rises on Q1 beat; buys Canadian AI-based clinical reference firm
data.description:
Doximity (NYSE:DOCS) added ~8% in the premarket on Friday after the company posted better-than-expected financials for Q1 fiscal 2026 and announced the acquisition of Pathway Medical, a Canadian startup focused on AI and evidence-based clinical reference.

The San Francisco, California-headquartered online medical platform reported $145.9M in revenue for the quarter, ahead of the top end of its guidance range and exceeding the consensus by $6.4M. The company recorded ~15% YoY topline growth thanks to a 118% net revenue retention rate on a trailing 12-month basis.

Meanwhile, DOCS’ adj. net income climbed ~29% YoY to $71.9M, topping Street forecasts by $0.05 per share, as its non-GAAP net income margin improved 510 bps to 49.2%.

Looking ahead, the company projected $157M - $158M and $628M - $636M in revenue for Q2 and fiscal 2026, ahead of the $150.3M and $626.3M projected by analysts, respectively.

As for the Pathway Medical acquisition, Doximity (NYSE:DOCS) said that the deal closed on July 29 for $26M in cash and equity grants worth up to $37M.
